_readme: Per-kernel input/expectation specs for the aiesim breadth sweep
(scripts/aiesim-sweep.sh -> crates/xdna-emu-ffi/tests/aiesim_sweep.rs).
The generic sweep seeds EVERY buffer with in[i]=i. That is wrong for kernels
whose arguments must carry structured input -- notably control-packet kernels,
where one argument is a stream of control packets (header + parity + payload).
Garbage in that slot fails the parity check, the packets are dropped, the
kernel's locks/reads never resolve, and the runtime-sequence dma_wait hangs.
That hang is a harness artifact, not an emulator/aiesim bug.
A kernel listed here overrides the generic seeding per-argument and (where an
'expect' is given) is checked for correctness. Kernels NOT listed keep the
generic i-pattern behavior unchanged.
SCHEMA
kernels.<name>.args[]   one entry per runtime-sequence argument that needs
non-generic handling (DdrPatch arg order == alloc order)
idx     int           argument index (0-based)
role    string        'ctrl_in' | 'output' | 'generic'
ctrl_in -> seed buffer with the encoded packet words
output  -> zero-seed; verified if 'expect' present
generic -> i-pattern seed (same as unlisted args)
packets []            (ctrl_in only) high-level packet ops, encoded to u32
words by the runner. Header layout per AM025 control
packet format: stream_id<<24 | op<<22 | (beats-1)<<20
| addr, with even-popcount parity in bit 31.
op        string    'write' | 'read'
addr      hex-str   tile-local register/memory address (e.g. "0x1F000")
data      int|[int] (write) payload word(s); beats = number of words
stream_id int       (default 0) packet stream id
beats     int       (read, default 1) logical beat count (response words)
expect  object        (output only) expected buffer contents
base    int         value of element 0
count   int         number of elements to check
stride  int         (default 1) step between consecutive elements
modulo  int         (optional) wrap the index: expected[i]=base+stride*(i%modulo)
Sources: header encoding + parity mirror mlir-aie's npu-xrt add_one_ctrl_packet
test.cpp (Apache-2.0); reproduced here as behavioral facts, not copied code.
